diagrams.title: Multilayer Feedforward Network Architecture
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a fully connected multilayer feedforward neural network showing the input layer, multiple hidden layers, and output layer. This diagram illustrates the fundamental structure where neurons in each layer connect to all neurons in the subsequent layer, demonstrating the 'feedforward' nature where information flows in one direction from input to output.
elements: Weighted connections between all adjacent layers
diagrams.purpose: Explains the basic architecture and terminology of multilayer feedforward networks, showing how depth (number of hidden layers) affects network capacity

diagrams.title: Forward Propagation Process
diagrams.description: A step-by-step visualization of the forward pass computation showing how input values transform through each layer. The diagram shows mathematical operations at each stage: weighted sum computation, bias addition, and activation function application. Arrows indicate the direction of data flow from input to output.
elements: Formula annotations (z = Wx + b, a = σ(z))
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how information flows and transforms through the network during inference, explaining the mathematical operations at each layer

diagrams.title: Network Depth vs. Layer Configuration
diagrams.description: A comparison diagram showing three different network configurations: shallow network (1 hidden layer), medium network (2 hidden layers), and deep network (3+ hidden layers). This visualization helps understand how network depth relates to model capacity and the concept of 'deep' learning.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the concept of network depth and how adding more hidden layers increases the network's ability to learn hierarchical representations
